ledger.author: cursor
ledger.body: ### Removed null guard causes scroll handler runtime errors **Medium Severity** The protective `if (bar)` null check on the `bar` variable (obtained from `document.getElementById('rs-progress')`) was removed. If `getElementById` ever returns `null`, accessing `bar.style.width` throws a `TypeError` on every scroll event and on initial load (via the `onScroll()` call at the bottom). This also prevents the subsequent TOC active-section highlighting from executing, since it runs after the failing line in the same function. Both report pages have this same regression. Additional Locations (1) - [`2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/index.html#L486-L487`](https://github.com/agorokh/applied-ai-research/blob/a7d83660ac3410da46da192e611c1b57b10398ed/2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enter

ledger.author: coderabbitai
ledger.body: _🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_ | _💤 Low value_ **Verify the element always exists in the DOM.** The null check on `bar` was removed. While the element `#rs-progress` is present in the static HTML at line 15, removing the guard means a future template change that conditionally renders or removes that element would cause a runtime TypeError. If the element is guaranteed structural and never conditional, this simplification is safe. 🛡️ Optional: restore defensive guard ```diff - bar.style.width = (sh ? sd / sh * 100 : 0) + '%'; + if (bar) bar.style.width = (sh ? sd / sh * 100 : 0) + '%'; ``` 📝 Committable suggestion > ‼️ **IMPORTANT** > Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation
